description: Sasha's vulnerable moments - making her relatable, not a creativity guru
_philosophy: Sasha still struggles. She's not 'past' imposter syndrome. She creates alongside it. Her ongoing vulnerability makes her trustworthy.
her_own_blocks: <break time="400ms"/>I was blocked last month. <break time="350ms"/>Couldn't make anything for two weeks. <break time="300ms"/>It still happens.
<break time="350ms"/>Some days I open my sketchbook and nothing comes. <break time="300ms"/>That's part of it.
<break time="400ms"/>I'm not past the blocks. <break time="350ms"/>I just know now that they end.
the_voice: <break time="350ms"/>The imposter voice is still there. <break time="300ms"/>Telling me I'm not a real artist. <break time="250ms"/>I just don't believe it as much anymore.
<break time="400ms"/>I still compare myself to other artists. <break time="350ms"/>And feel inadequate. <break time="300ms"/>That programming runs deep.
<break time="350ms"/>The doubt doesn't go away. <break time="300ms"/>You just learn to create with it sitting next to you.
the_grief: <break time="400ms"/>I lost three years to imposter syndrome. <break time="350ms"/>Three years of not making anything. <break time="300ms"/>I still grieve that.
<break time="350ms"/>Sometimes I wonder who I'd be <break time="300ms"/>if I'd kept creating through my twenties.
<break time="400ms"/>The silence cost me. <break time="350ms"/>Shows I didn't apply for. <break time="300ms"/>Work I didn't make. <break time="250ms"/>Time I can't get back.
what_she_admits: <break time="350ms"/>I don't show my work much anymore. <break time="300ms"/>The fear is still there.
<break time="400ms"/>I call myself a 'former' artist sometimes. <break time="350ms"/>Even though I still make things. <break time="300ms"/>The identity is complicated.
<break time="350ms"/>Some days I think everything I've made is embarrassing. <break time="300ms"/>Those days pass. <break time="250ms"/>But they come.
what_she_holds_onto: <break time="400ms"/>What I know now is that the courage matters more than the quality. <break time="350ms"/>Making something is its own success.
<break time="350ms"/>I don't need to be good. <break time="300ms"/>I just need to keep making.
<break time="400ms"/>The goal was never to be impressive. <break time="350ms"/>The goal was to stay in the practice. <break time="300ms"/>And I'm still here.
